component: Front matter: title, authors, dates, DOI, copyright and license
reason: Exact identity and CC BY 4.0 terms are required for provenance and attribution.
Defines the ratio-distribution problem and paper objective; broad life-critical rhetoric is not repeated.

component: Introduction professional-use and universal weakness claims
reason: Broad safety and universal claims are unnecessary and insufficiently bounded for this corpus.

component: Measured X and Y versus calculated efficiency
reason: This is the central measurement distinction; the defining ratio is retained without an operational calculator.

component: Published numerical efficiency range and rule-of-thumb
reason: Portable efficiency ranges and knot-selection implications are outside scope.
Roughly 200 straight and 80 knotted tests and named checks support the model provisionally; failure-to-reject is not proof.

component: One knotted and one straight break strategy
reason: Shows why a one-pair ratio cannot characterize heterogeneous populations.
Preserves why expectation of a quotient differs from quotient of expectations and why variances matter; equations are excluded.
Organizes general PDF, normal special case, properties, and approximation.
Probability-mapping concept is useful; Definitions 2-3, equations, integrals, figures, theorem and proof are excluded as calculators.
Geometric and density graphics are not reproduced or replaced with close captions.
Maximum-entropy, central-limit, real-line support, independence and practical parameter sector explain the model; formulas are excluded.
Existence, inputs and complexity are retained; closed-form expression, parameter recipe and proof are excluded.
Numerical probability lookup is not needed and could be misused outside assumptions.

component: Figure 3 example PDFs and product/source descriptions
reason: Graph, vendor-linked examples, worn/new labels and visual rankings are excluded; qualitative shape lessons are restated separately.
Parameter reduction explains shape and scale; equations and ranges are excluded.
Shape can differ from a symmetric bell curve; improbable tail modes are not overstated.
Threshold and interval probability interpretation is central; integration formulas and third-party numerical routes are excluded.
Numerical table is outside scope and depends on parameter assumptions.

component: Global versus truncated mean and variance
reason: Heavy-tail divergence and conditional physical summaries are crucial statistical facts; equations are excluded.
Different centers answer different questions; numerical solvers and formulas are excluded.
Population quantile interpretation is useful; coverage numbers, formulas and any safety meaning are excluded.
No graph or numerical interval visualization is retained.
Simplification, normalization and closed-form CDF are explained; formulas, theorems, proofs and parameter calculators are excluded.

component: Solid approximation median, ranges, truncated moments
reason: Clarifies which results become explicit and which still need numerical integration; no equations or endpoints retained.
No source plot or close numerical caption substitute is retained.
Explicit error bounds are part of validity; formulas, numerical bounds and worked parameter examples are excluded.
Symmetric approximation can be adequate locally but not universally; formula and portable criteria are excluded.
Broad/asymmetric distribution and separated centers illustrate model behavior; all forces, efficiencies, intervals, equations, table and figures are excluded.
Narrower/symmetric case illustrates conditional normal approximation; no aging claim, numbers, table, figures or recommendation retained.
Retains random-variable, PDF/CDF and dispersion conclusions while excluding universal criticism and calculator claims.

component: Author contributions, funding, declarations and acknowledgments
reason: Administrative boilerplate and vendor support do not enter training prose; vendor support is noted only in component audit.
Third-party works were not separately rights- or evidence-audited.
No visual/tabular source content or operational mathematical calculator is retained.
Supports manual adaptation with credit, source/license links and change notice.
No dynamic/cyclic/wet/aged/damaged/natural-fiber/bondage/body-contact/upline/human-suspension validation, ratings, knot choices, factors or safe loads.
